itunes_results.name: Mint (discontinued)
itunes_results.category: budgeting and expense tracker — bank account linking, automated categorization, bill tracking, credit score monitoring; Mint was a TRACKER (Intuit shut it down Jan 2024 after 16 years — 532K ratings proves the market existed). Its successor Credit Karma has 1.3M App Store ratings. Neither teaches personal finance concepts daily: why compound interest is the eighth wonder of the world and exactly what it does to a $5K credit card balance over 10 years, why the 4% safe withdrawal rate was derived from the Trinity Study and what it actually means for your retirement math, or why most Americans have negative net worth due to lifestyle inflation outpacing income growth

itunes_results.name: YNAB (You Need A Budget)
itunes_results.category: zero-based budgeting app — envelope budgeting method, debt payoff tools, real-time sync, partner sharing; budgeting METHODOLOGY tool (at $14.99/mo) not a daily personal finance science streak teaching why the debt avalanche saves more money than the debt snowball despite the snowball's psychological advantage, why increasing your savings rate by 1% has a nonlinear impact on your retirement date via compound interest acceleration, or why the 50/30/20 budget rule was specifically calibrated for median-income households and breaks down at higher incomes

itunes_results.name: Copilot
itunes_results.category: AI-powered budget app — smart categorization, net worth tracking, investment monitoring, spending insights; premium AI budget tracker NOT a daily financial literacy streak teaching why net worth is the only financial metric that matters (not income), why insurance is negative expected value that's still always worth buying (risk transfer math), or why tax-advantaged accounts (401K, IRA, HSA) create asymmetric returns that after-tax accounts can never match

daily_personal_finance_fundamentals_stre.notes: 23M personal finance community — one of the largest Reddit communities on any topic. The /r/personalfinance wiki has been read millions of times. Yet every major finance app is a TRACKER. Nobody teaches personal finance principles daily in a streak format. Dave Ramsey has sold 12M books. Ramit Sethi's 'I Will Teach You To Be Rich' is a bestseller. The market HUNGERS for financial education. Yet no streak app delivers one daily financial concept: the Rule of 72, the debt avalanche vs snowball math, the actual impact of fees on index funds, the 4% rule mechanics. Financial literacy is a certified gap — 2/3 of Americans can't pass a basic financial literacy test. >>> DIRECT BUILD: daily personal finance fundamentals streak. Score: 26. P0.
